paper_id: Piest et al. - 2024 - A Domain Reference Ontology for Design Science Research Knowledge Bases

models.model_label: Domain Reference Ontology for DSR Knowledge Bases (OntoUML model, with operational OWL ontology)

evidence_quotes.quote: An operational ontology was designed and implemented in a DSR project in OWL using WebProtégé.
Building upon the OntoUML models in Visual Paradigm, the gUFO export option generated a .ttl file. This file was imported into desktop Protégé and used to create an OWL ontology.
Upon the UFO and OntoUML, an OLED can automatically generate an operational OWL ontology based on OntoUML, which can contribute to shortening the implementation phase.

goals.justification: The OntoUML domain reference ontology was actively used to generate and implement an operational OWL ontology (an artifact/system); the model directly enabled creation of the operational ontology, matching the For Intervening signals 'implemented', 'used to create', 'generated'.

evidence_quotes.quote: More specifically, the domain reference ontology for DSR KBs aims to create a common language and vocabulary, classify types of knowledge, and provide a

goals.justification: The stated purpose is to create a common language/vocabulary and classify types of knowledge — explicit signals of 'clarify', 'define', 'conceptualize' that map directly to For Understanding (building shared domain knowledge about DSR KBs).

evidence_quotes.quote: Knowledge bases play an important role in Design Science Research (DSR).

The domain reference ontology provides an abstract blueprint for DSR knowledge bases and a conceptual foundation to develop operational ontologies to support the cumulative development of design knowledge.
The project repository will contribute to making the results of the DSR project FAIR and contribute to the reuse and cumulative development of design knowledge [5].
Desktop Protégé provides functionality to generate interactive documentation as an OWL Doc.

goals.justification: The ontology is explicitly framed as a reusable reference/knowledge base and blueprint to be consulted asynchronously by other scholars and professionals for cumulative development of design knowledge — matches Documenting signals 'documentation', 'knowledge base', 'reference', 'reuse', 'repository'.

evidence_quotes.quote: A user and working group will be established to manage the lifecycle, which is open for interested scholars and professionals to join.
Another advantage of WebProtégé is that it facilitates online collaboration.

uncertain_goals.reason: References to stakeholders, collaboration, and user/working groups suggest possible communicative use, but the paper does not explicitly describe the model being used in meetings or live discussion to align distinct groups; the framing is closer to documentation and shared reference.

evidence_quotes.quote: Use 2b: Designers produce solution designs for applications to address/solve problems using the KB.

Use 6a: System users query the KB for inquiry (e.g., explore knowledge, search), analytical purposes (e.g., bibliometric analysis), and the development of software agents (e.g., recommender or assistant to support users 1-4).

uncertain_goals.reason: Some intended uses describe analytical/decision-supporting queries and producing solution designs, but the evidence is intended/future use rather than realized problem-solving with the model itself, and overlaps with For Intervening (using the KB to build artifacts).
